content_markdown: An astronaut orbits the Earth in a space capsule. Which statement is correct?

diagram_metadata.description: No diagram provided in the image.

options.label: A
options.text: There are no gravitational forces acting on the space capsule or the astronaut.

options.label: B
options.text: The space capsule and the astronaut each have the same acceleration.

options.label: C
options.text: The space capsule and the astronaut are each in equilibrium.

options.label: D
options.text: The gravitational force on the space capsule is equal to that on the astronaut.

vibe_explanation: In orbit, both the astronaut and the capsule are in free-fall. Since they are at the same distance from the center of the Earth, they experience the same gravitational field strength $g = \frac{GM}{r^2}$, and thus have the same centripetal acceleration. They are not in equilibrium because there is a net force (gravity) acting on them, and the gravitational force $F = mg$ differs because their masses are different.
